novel_features.name: Retention benchmark
novel_features.description: See whether an app's retention curve is normal for its genre by comparing it against the cohort median of its tag.
novel_features.rationale: Requires tag-taxonomy cohort selection, one batched charts requests[] pull, and a locally computed median that no single API call returns.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when a retention estimate needs context before it goes in a report; single-app numbers are widely distrusted without a cohort baseline.

novel_features.name: Chart diff
novel_features.description: See who entered, dropped, or moved in any top chart between two synced snapshots.
novel_features.rationale: The charts API is stateless; the local SQLite snapshot history makes movement queries possible at all.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this for the Monday movers report instead of hand-diffing two CSV exports.

novel_features.name: Soft-launch radar
novel_features.description: Find newly detected soft-launch titles with first-seen dates per test market.
novel_features.rationale: The soft-launches endpoint only returns today's list; first-seen detection needs the local snapshot history.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to scout new titles entering test markets without remembering what was there last week.

novel_features.name: Entitlements probe
novel_features.description: Discover exactly which AppMagic endpoint groups your contract includes, before a bare 403 surprises you.
novel_features.rationale: No API endpoint reports plan entitlements; the probe synthesizes a per-group access map into the local store for doctor and error messages to read.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Run this first on a new account; AppMagic endpoint groups are contract-gated and tier limits are not published.

novel_features.name: Competitor watchlist report
novel_features.description: Pull downloads, revenue, and retention for your saved competitor set in one side-by-side table.
novel_features.rationale: Combines a persistent local watchlist with the API's batched charts requests[] envelope to replace N per-app pulls.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this for the weekly competitor deck; the watchlist remembers the set so the agent only asks once.

novel_features.name: Tag rollup
novel_features.description: Aggregate market size (summed downloads or revenue) across one or more genre tags.
novel_features.rationale: The API has no cross-tag aggregation endpoint; this joins the synced taxonomy, tops, and one batched charts call locally.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this for genre sizing questions like 'how big is match-3 in Japan' that otherwise need a spreadsheet.

novel_features.name: ASO movers
novel_features.description: See which keywords an app gained, lost, or moved on between two dates.
novel_features.rationale: The ASO/ASA endpoints return positions, never deltas; the mover set is computed locally from two series pulls.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this for weekly keyword reports; pass --dataset asa for Apple Search Ads terms.

novel_features.name: Live-ops overlay
novel_features.description: Correlate a competitor's live-ops events with their revenue and download movement.
novel_features.rationale: Joins the live-ops event endpoints with charts series by date window; live-ops data at API level is unique to AppMagic.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to answer 'did that event spike their revenue' without eyeballing two dashboards.

novel_features.name: Web: monetization offers
novel_features.description: Browse AppMagic's in-game IAP offer library (structure, duration, pricing) from the web app's Monetization Intelligence module.
novel_features.rationale: This dataset has no official-API equivalent; the command replays the web app's XHR surface with a browser-session token.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this only with APPMAGIC_WEB_TOKEN set; it is an unofficial surface that can change without notice.

novel_features.name: Web: hourly top charts
novel_features.description: Intraday top-chart rankings with rank-change diffs from the web app surface.
novel_features.rationale: The official API has no hourly granularity; only the web XHR surface exposes it.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when daily charts are too coarse, e.g. launch-day tracking; requires APPMAGIC_WEB_TOKEN.

novel_features.name: Web: live-ops tag counts
novel_features.description: Market-level counts of live-ops events grouped by tag for a store and country.
novel_features.rationale: The web app's feature-intelligence aggregation has no official-API equivalent.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to benchmark how common an event type (gacha, daily, competitive) is across the market; requires APPMAGIC_WEB_TOKEN.

novel_features.name: Web: tag app counts
novel_features.description: Number of apps per taxonomy tag for market-niche sizing.
novel_features.rationale: Tag population counts are only exposed on the web XHR surface.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to gauge how crowded a niche is before deeper sizing with tag-rollup; requires APPMAGIC_WEB_TOKEN.

narrative.headline: Full AppMagic API coverage plus chart history, deltas, and entitlement maps.
narrative.value_prop: AppMagic's official API is 117 stateless operations behind HTTP Basic auth; the only public client anywhere is a stale R wrapper. This CLI covers the full surface with typed commands and adds what statelessness cannot: snapshot history for chart-diff and soft-launch-radar, a persistent competitor watchlist, cohort-median retention benchmarking, cross-tag market rollups, and an entitlements probe that maps exactly which endpoint groups your contract includes.
narrative.auth_narrative: AppMagic has no separate API token: the API authenticates with your account login and password over HTTP Basic. Set APPMAGIC_LOGIN and APPMAGIC_PASSWORD in your environment. API access itself is a sales-led contract feature, and endpoint groups (Steam, ASA, contacts, period comparison) are entitlement-gated per contract; run 'entitlements' to map what your account includes. The optional 'web' command group uses a different credential: APPMAGIC_WEB_TOKEN, the Bearer token a logged-in appmagic.rocks browser session stores in localStorage under 'datamagic.token'. That surface is unofficial and the token expires periodically.

quickstart.comment: Health check: config and connectivity work before any credentials are set
Mirror the 500+ tag genre taxonomy locally so tag IDs resolve offline
Map which endpoint groups your contract includes before relying on any of them
After two runs on different days, see who entered or dropped in the chart
The weekly competitor pull as one batched call instead of N tabs

troubleshoots.fix: Set APPMAGIC_LOGIN and APPMAGIC_PASSWORD (your AppMagic account credentials; there is no separate API token). Confirm your contract includes API access.

troubleshoots.symptom: 403 forbidden on steam, asa, contacts, or period-comparison commands
troubleshoots.fix: That endpoint group is not in your contract. Run `appmagic-pp-cli entitlements --refresh` to map your access; contact AppMagic to extend the contract.
You hit the plan's daily quota or concurrency cap. Back off and retry after the quota resets; quotas are plan-dependent (the API returns X-RateLimit-* headers and Retry-After on 429).

These read snapshot history that the commands capture themselves: run the command on two different days (each run stores a snapshot), or pass --from/--to to pick stored dates. `sync` does not populate these tables.
APPMAGIC_WEB_TOKEN expired. Log into appmagic.rocks in a browser and copy the token from localStorage key 'datamagic.token'.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when an agent needs mobile or Steam market intelligence from AppMagic: competitor downloads/revenue/retention pulls, top-chart movement, soft-launch scouting, genre sizing across the tag taxonomy, ASO/ASA keyword deltas, live-ops correlation, or SDK adoption. It shines for recurring analyst rituals where local snapshot history and a persistent watchlist compound over time.
anti_triggers: Do not use this CLI for first-party analytics of your own app (use App Store Connect, Google Play Console, or your MMP; AppMagic figures are model estimates)
Do not use it to scrape app stores directly; it only queries AppMagic's API with your contract's entitlements
Do not expect web-app-only modules (custom dashboards, Feature Library UI screens, App Tracker alerts) beyond the four documented 'web' commands
Do not use estimate data as ground truth for financial reporting; estimates are modeled, not measured

recipes.title: Monday movers report
recipes.explanation: Entered/dropped/moved in the US iPhone grossing chart since the previous snapshot, agent-shaped for a standup summary.

recipes.title: Scout test markets
recipes.explanation: First-seen soft-launch titles across the classic test markets in the last 30 days.

recipes.title: Genre heat check
recipes.explanation: Summed 30-day revenue for two genres in Japan without a spreadsheet. Totals cover the top N apps per tag (default --top 20), not the whole market.

recipes.title: Bounded competitor pull for agents
recipes.command: appmagic-pp-cli watchlist report --country US --metrics downloads,revenue --since 7d --agent --select apps.name,apps.downloads,apps.revenue
recipes.explanation: Watchlist metrics narrowed to three dotted fields so a deeply nested batch response does not flood agent context.

recipes.title: Sanity-check a retention number
recipes.explanation: The app's curve against its genre cohort median at D1/D7/D30.

trigger_phrases: top grossing games in the US
who entered the top charts this week
how big is the match-3 market
